decision.decision_question: Which player promise should receive the first playable prototype?

options.main_risk: May not prove the core play value

decision.current_default_action: Prototype option B because it is cheaper

utility_model.objective: Learn which promise justifies the next prototype investment
utility_model.decision_delta_if_wrong: One sprint and a misleading concept direction
assumptions: A paper prototype can expose whether option A produces meaningful agency

uncertainty_map.uncertainty: Players can understand and value the systemic agency promise in a minimal interaction

candidate_information_actions.action: Run an eight-player paper-prototype test focused on one agency decision

expected_signals.signal: At least six players predict the consequence and ask to try another strategy
Most players cannot explain why their choice matters
expected_signals.posterior_update: Agency promise or presentation is weaker than assumed
expected_signals.action_if_seen: Revise option A or prototype option B

candidate_information_actions.reliability_and_bias: Small convenience sample; useful for comprehension and desire, not market-size claims
candidate_information_actions.evpi_upper_bound: No more than the avoided cost of one misdirected prototype sprint
candidate_information_actions.evsi_estimate: Medium to high because the decision is near the boundary

selected_probe.why_smallest_high_value_probe: It can reverse the prototype choice without building either full option
selected_probe.sample_or_evidence_gate: Eight sessions; stop early only for a repeated blocking misunderstanding

stop_when: The preferred option is robust across the observed response range
The eight-session evidence gate is reached
stop_rule.fallback_action: Use the current default action and record residual uncertainty

outcome.prior: Option B is the default because it is cheaper
